activities_projects.title: Mapping Meena’s Dreams
activities_projects.objective: Identify the protagonist's aspirations and challenges
steps: Read the story 'The Day the River Spoke' carefully
Identify Meena’s main dream regarding school and the river
Draw a small cloud in your notebook and write her dream inside
List two things that make it difficult for her to go to school
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand character motivation and conflict
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear identification of dreams and obstacles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Personifying the River
activities_projects.objective: Understand the literary device of personification used in the title
steps: Find lines in the story where the river is described as if it is alive
Write down what the river 'says' or 'whispers' to Meena
Imagine you are the river; write one sentence telling Meena to be brave
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students grasp the concept of personification in a simple way
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance of quotes and creative sentence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Journey to School: Sequencing
activities_projects.objective: Order the main events of Meena's efforts to learn
steps: Identify 4 key moments from the start to the end of the story
Write them down in the correct chronological order
Use transition words like 'First', 'Then', 'Next', and 'Finally'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to sequence narrative events
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct chronological order and use of connectors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Nature’s Vocabulary
activities_projects.objective: Learn descriptive words related to the river and village life
steps: Find 5 'nature words' in the chapter (e.g., ripple, bank, flow)
Write their meanings in simple English
Draw a small icon or picture next to each word to show its meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand their descriptive and environmental vocabulary
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and visual representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Meena’s Diary Entry
steps: Imagine you are Meena after your first day of learning
Write 4–5 sentences about how you felt looking at the river
Mention one person who helped or inspired you
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice expressing emotions through writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Emotional depth and clarity of expression (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dialogue with the Teacher
steps: Create a short 3-line dialogue between Meena and a teacher
Meena should ask a question about the 'black marks' (letters) in a book
The teacher should give an encouraging reply
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand basic punctuation and flow in dialogues
activities_projects.evaluation: Proper use of quotation marks and tone (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identifying Action Words (Verbs)
activities_projects.objective: Identify verbs that describe the river and Meena’s actions
steps: Find 3 verbs that describe what the river does
Find 3 verbs that describe what Meena does
Write them in two separate columns in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen their understanding of verbs in context
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of action words (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion: Why is Education Important?
activities_projects.objective: Connect the story’s theme to real-life importance of school
steps: Discuss in groups: 'Why did Meena want to go to school so badly?'
Write down two reasons on a piece of paper
Present your reasons to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and speaking skills

activities_projects.title: Sensory Description
activities_projects.objective: Describe the setting using the five senses
steps: Find one thing Meena sees, one thing she hears, and one thing she touches
Write a descriptive sentence for each using adjectives from the book
Example: 'The cool, wet sand between her toes.'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to use sensory details in writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Effective use of adjectives and sensory focus (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Poster: Learning Together
steps: Draw a scene showing Meena by the river with a book
Write a slogan at the bottom about 'The Joy of Learning'
Decorate your poster and share it with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students synthesize the story's message through art
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the theme (10 marks)
